notes: Global tech rout: Nasdaq futures -3%, chips routed worldwide, Micron -9%; VIX up to 20.1 with PCE inflation data looming. Open day P&L already -$54.9k, so flipping to risk-off at 0.30 and pulling chip names from favor — the prior leaders (MU/MRVL/SNDK/NBIS) are now the epicenter of the selloff.
journal_entry: Big change from yesterday's neutral/0.50: overnight chip selloff routed semis globally (Micron -9%), VIX 17.3->20.1, and account opened down ~$55k. Dropped risk to 0.30 risk-off and moved former chip favorites to avoid; only defensive DTE and resilient AAL look constructive. Watching PCE data and whether chips stabilize before re-risking — let winners ride, no buying into falling knives.
